title: Write-set admission control (Phase 3.2)

title: Frozen evaluator with content hash pinning (Phase 3.3)

title: Bounded rounds with mandatory reflection (Phase 3.4)
description: Mission contracts declare round budget. After each round, ethos forces structured reflection step before next round can start. title: Independent verification — verifier isolation (Phase 3.5)

SubagentStart hook strips parent transcript. Depends on 3.1. See ETHOS-ROADMAP.md §3.5.

title: fix: Linux hook stdin hang — shell reads stdin, not Go (DES-029)
description: Ethos hooks hung silently on Linux. Claude Code spawns hooks via /bin/sh -c, making /dev/stdin inaccessible to the Go binary. All 6 hook scripts now use bash read -t 1 + printf to normalize stdin over a fresh pipe. Includes Go-level readWithTimeout defense-in-depth, subprocess integration tests, fake Claude spawn regression test (Node.js), Linux proc tests, and DES-029/DES-030 ADRs.

title: No subcommand-level --help flag parsing
allow_empty_write_set archetype flag is not enforced — Contract.Validate rule 11 rejects empty write_set unconditionally
description: Archetype YAML declares allow_empty_write_set: true for 'report' and 'inbox' archetypes, meaning contracts of those types should be allowed to have an empty write_set. But Contract.Validate() rule 11 rejects empty write_set for ALL contracts regardless of type. The flag is parsed into the Archetype model but not consumed by the validator. Either (a) wire allow_empty_write_set into Validate() as a type-aware exemption, or (b) remove the flag from the archetype schema and require all contracts to have a non-empty write_set. Current workaround in built-in pipelines: report-archetype stages use .tmp/*.md report paths. Needs design decision + implementation.

title: MCP tool consolidation: method-dispatch pattern
description: Consolidate MCP tools using GitHub's method-dispatch pattern: one tool per resource with a method enum for verbs. skill(method: create|list|show|add|remove), personality(method: create|list|show|set), writing_style(method: create|list|show|set), ext(method: get|set|del|list). Also add slash command wrappers for all MCP tools. 13 attribute tools → 3, 4 ext tools → 1.

title: mission: TOCTOU between checkVerifierHash Load and renderVerifierBlock ReadFile
description: djb's Phase 3.5 frozen-evaluator review (ethos-07m.9) flagged a potential TOCTOU: Phase 3.3's checkVerifierHash does a lockless Store.Load of every matching open mission, and Phase 3.5's renderVerifierBlock then does a second lockless os.ReadFile of the same contract to get byte-for-byte bytes for the isolation block. A concurrent Store.Update between the two reads would inject contract bytes that differ from what the hash gate saw. Phase 3.5's threat model excludes adversarial concurrent mutations (the operator editing their own contract mid-spawn is the leader, not an attacker), and the pattern is inherited from Phase 3.3's existing lockless Load discipline. Not a blocker for 3.5 per djb's verdict. Fix options: (a) thread the contract bytes through checkVerifierHash so the first read's bytes are reused, or (b) add per-mission flocks around the read chain. File for Phase 3.6+ consideration if concurrent-update resistance becomes a threat-model concern.

title: team.ValidateStructural: human-readable member/collaboration identifiers in error messages
description:
Follow-up from ethos-2z2 round 1 mdm review. Current error format uses numeric indices: 'member 14: duplicate assignment (alice, dev)'. A user with a 20-member team has to count YAML list entries to find member 14. YAML lists are not line-numbered by tooling, so this is manual counting.

Suggested: keep the numeric prefix, ADD a name parenthetical where available: 'member 14 (alice/dev): duplicate assignment'. Falls back to the bare index for empty-field cases where there's no name to show.

Blocked in-bead by the ethos-2z2 byte-equality lock. Changing the format breaks TestValidate_DelegatesToStructural byte-for-byte anchor, breaks any existing caller grepping error text, and requires a coordinated update.

The duplicate-assignment error is already an exemplar (it names the tuple). The others could learn from it.

Source: mdm round 1 review of commit 83c45be on fix/team-load-validation.

title: Store.AppendResult/AppendReflection rollback writes empty file instead of removing
description: When AppendResult (or AppendReflection) rolls back the sibling file after an event-log append failure on a brand-new mission, the rollback calls writeResultsLocked (or writeReflectionsLocked) with an empty slice, producing results: null on disk instead of os.Remove-ing the file. Close gate and append-only invariants are still correct, but a stat check for file existence now sees an empty file where the original state was no file. Class fix across both AppendResult and AppendReflection. Add a small helper (removeResultsLocked / removeReflectionsLocked) that os.Remove-s and ignores os.IsNotExist. Add a sabotage test asserting file absence after rollback. Phase 3.4 reflection store has the identical pattern; fix both in one PR. Found by silent-failure-hunter during Phase 3.6 round 1 review. See feedback from djb, mdm, and feature-dev:code-reviewer on commit 83bb83a.

title: Design: agency levels for personas — mission vs. design mode
description: Ethos binds one identity to one role with fixed boundaries. But the same agent (e.g., bwk) should exercise low agency in a mission (execute the spec) and high agency in design work (propose alternatives, push back, reshape). The mission contract constrains scope but doesn't change how the agent thinks. Options to evaluate: (1) agency field on mission contract (low/medium/high), (2) multiple roles per identity (go-specialist vs go-architect, different tools and responsibilities), (3) mission-mode vs autonomous-mode (contract presence = constraint, absence = full agency). Must work with Claude Code's agent harness. Key tension: agents have domain expertise and judgment — suppressing that in all contexts is self-defeating. Filed from CEO observation: 'only making them do what you tell them seems self-defeating.'
close_reason: Resolved: agency is expressed through contract specificity, not a new field. Implementation missions have tight write-sets and specific criteria. Design missions have broad write-sets and open-ended criteria (e.g., 'evaluate three approaches'). Same contract schema, same persona — the leader decides the structure level when writing the mission. No code change needed. Document in PR/FAQ and mission examples.

title: mission: verify files_changed added/removed counts against git diff --numstat
description:
The mission result schema accepts the worker's byte counts without cross-checking them against the real git diff. A worker could ship inaccurate accounting by mistake or deliberately. Not a bug (workers are trusted in the permission model), but it's a free correctness check the primitive could run before the result lands.

Proposal: add a '--verify' flag to 'ethos mission result' that, when set:
1. Runs 'git diff --numstat HEAD~1' (or 'git diff --numstat' if no commit yet)
2. For each files_changed entry, compares added/removed against the real numstat output
3. Rejects the submission if the declared counts don't match
4. Optional: auto-fill added/removed when the worker submits zeros (convenience, not correctness)

Off by default to preserve submit-without-commit workflows; on by default once adopted. Errors should name the file and the discrepancy.

Surfaced during Phase 3 dogfood (PR #199). bwk flagged it as friction observation 4 in round 2.
close_reason: Mission result --verify flag shipped. Cross-checks files_changed added/removed counts against git diff --numstat before result lands. PR #201, squash merge 277cd90. Five commits: initial impl with six subprocess tests (2ea91c4), testGitEnv consistency (7288228), path canonicalization via exported CanonicalPath reusing splitSegments (768a947), --end-of-options security fix preventing base-flag injection (049a356), schema-precedence validation + --no-renames for rename handling (10050c8). Copilot caught the path canonicalization gap and the validation-precedence issue; Bugbot caught the --end-of-options injection and independently the rename issue. Twelve verify tests total, all subprocess-style against real temp git repos.

title: mission: document write_set-as-envelope convention in delegation template
description:
The contract's write_set is a permission envelope — the upper bound of what the worker may touch — not a checklist of what the worker must touch. During Phase 3 dogfood round 2 (PR #199), bwk correctly did not touch three files that were in the write_set but didn't need changes. That's the right behavior, but the distinction wasn't explicit in the contract shape.

Proposal: adopt a convention where contract.inputs.files is the 'expected blast radius' (what the leader thinks the worker will touch) and contract.write_set is the 'permission envelope' (the maximum the worker is allowed to touch). Workers surface the delta in their result.prose.

Scope:
1. Document the convention in the ethos mission help text
2. Update the delegation template I use to distinguish the two fields
3. Consider (but do not require) a schema convention where inputs.files is a subset of write_set (validated at create time)

Depends on ethos-cqt (escalate discoverability) because both update the delegation template and mission help. Should ship in the same PR or immediately adjacent.
close_reason: Write_set convention documented in ethos mission create --help. inputs.files is read-context references; write_set is the permission envelope for writes. Workers surface what they actually changed in result.files_changed. PR #202, squash merge 94ca8ac.

title: team.Store.Load should call Validate — close the referential-integrity class
description:
Discovered during ethos-9ai.1 round-3 silent-failure hunt (PR #TBD). The anti-responsibility generator's round-2 fix warned on typo'd collaboration Type values but is silent on typo'd From values:

Collaboration{From: "go-speciallist", To: "coo", Type: "reports_to"} never matches any role (typo in From), deriveAntiResponsibilities returns nil, section is omitted, no signal to user.

The root cause: team.Store.Load() does not call team.Validate(). Referential integrity is enforced at Save() time but not Load() time. Every consumer of Store.Load has to re-implement the same checks and will drift — the anti-responsibility generator has a subset (Type validation only), other consumers have none.

Fix: make team.Store.Load run structural validation (the portion that does not need identity/role existence callbacks — c.From/c.To being filled by a team member, c.Type in validCollabTypes). Consumers needing the identity/role existence check can call the full Validate separately.

Impact: closes silent-failure hunter HIGH #B from ethos-9ai.1 round 3, plus removes duplicated validation in the anti-responsibility generator (LOW #G).

Source: internal/team/store.go:97 (Load), internal/team/team.go:83-107 (Validate).

References feedback_fix_the_class_not_the_instance.md — fix the store, not the consumer.
close_reason: Shipped via PR punt-labs/ethos#193 (merged 2026-04-09, commit 152fcb7). team.Store.Load enforces structural invariants via ValidateStructural split — split team.Validate into ValidateStructural (pure, no callbacks) and Validate (structural + identity/role existence callbacks). Closes the silent-drop class from silent-failure hunter HIGH #B in 9ai.1 round 3 (typo'd collaboration.from silently matched zero members). 3 bwk rounds + 1 local 4-reviewer cycle (all 4 approved with test-layer findings only, no production defects) + 1 Copilot PR cycle (1 inline: wrong errors.Is reference in test comment, fixed in r3). Binary verification: happy path md5 byte-identical across all rounds; error path exits 1 with 5-layer chain 'ethos hook session-start: generating agents: loading team "typo-team": validating team "typo-team": collaboration 0: role "go-speciallist" not filled by any member'. Follow-up P3 beads filed: ethos-zfp (list valid types in invalid-type error), ethos-29t (human-readable member/collaboration identifiers), ethos-lu3 (replace jfreeman fixture filler with dedicated placeholder). All three blocked in-bead by the byte-equality lock.

description: All five mission write commands (create, result, reflect, advance, close) exit 0 with zero stdout on success. create especially needs to echo the mission_id so pipelines can chain subsequent commands without a follow-up list. Minimal fix: print 'created m-YYYY-MM-DD-NNN' (or similar) to stdout after each successful write. Honors --json flag for structured consumers. Found during Phase 3 mission primitive smoke test, 2026-04-09.
close_reason: Mission write commands now echo operation outcome on stdout in text mode (JSON unchanged). All 5 commands (create, result, reflect, advance, close) emit a one-line verb: id k=v summary so callers can chain without a follow-up show/list. Shipped as PR #200, squash merge 705b49d. Five commits driven by reviewer findings: initial echo (6d32b1c), close JSON expansion + JSON tests (42962f7), README/CHANGELOG sync (28eca0d), nil-check defensive fix (871dc24), Store.Close refactor to eliminate post-commit re-read race (cee2d51). The Store.Close signature now returns (*Result, error) so the CLI gets the satisfying result without re-reading after the lock releases — eliminates a TOCTOU window and shortens runMissionClose by 21 lines. Follow-up bead ethos-w42 filed for widening the MCP handleCloseMission response to match the CLI JSON shape.

title: Integration design: ethos + beadle, ethos + biff, ethos + quarry
description:
Design and implement the integration between ethos and three consumer systems. Critical constraint: ethos must NOT know about beadle, biff, or quarry — dependency flows one way. Consumers use ext/ to store their own data on identities.

Three integrations:

1. ethos + beadle: Agents get email addresses and credentials via ext/beadle. Beadle reads identity (name, handle, email field) from ethos sidecar. Beadle stores GPG keys, IMAP config in ext/beadle. Result: agents can send/receive email as themselves.

2. ethos + biff: Agents get team messaging via ext/biff. Biff reads identity for display name and handle. Biff stores preferred TTY, routing preferences in ext/biff. Result: agents can /write, /read, /talk, /plan, /finger as themselves. Session roster already bridges ethos and biff — needs audit for completeness.

3. ethos + quarry: Agents get persistent memory across sessions. Quarry stores per-agent collection or namespace. Past conversations, decisions, work artifacts searchable as the agent's own memory. This is the least proven integration — quarry currently indexes project files, not agent memories. May need a new quarry feature (agent-scoped collections) or may work with existing registration + namespace conventions.

All three integrations must follow the sidecar contract: consumer reads ethos state from filesystem or MCP tools. Consumer stores its own data in ext/<namespace>. Ethos never imports or calls consumer code. Review punt-kit/standards/ for existing integration patterns and confirm or revise.

Deliverable: design doc per integration, implementation beads, ext schema definitions. No code changes to ethos core — only ext data and consumer-side code.

title: Archetype WriteSetConstraints loaded but not enforced — add lint heuristic
description: Archetype YAML files declare write_set_constraints (e.g., test archetype: *_test.go, testdata/**, docs/**, *.md). The Archetype model parses these into WriteSetConstraints []string. But NO lint heuristic checks contract write_set entries against the constraint patterns. The constraints are documentation-only today. Implementation needs: (1) a lint heuristic (probably H11 or higher) that loads the archetype for c.Type and checks each write_set entry against write_set_constraints patterns; (2) a custom doublestar-style matcher because Go's filepath.Match does not support ** (existing testdata/** and docs/** patterns assume doublestar semantics). Recommended: add the bmatcuk/doublestar dependency or write a custom matcher. Severity advisory (info) like other lint heuristics. Discovered during ethos-dr0 review.

comments.author: J F
comments.text: Built-in pipeline test stages use write_set: ["{target}"] which expands to directory paths like internal/foo/. These will fail test archetype write_set_constraints when enforcement ships. Consider either: (a) loosening the test archetype to accept directory paths (with understanding that enforcement becomes intent-only), or (b) changing pipeline test stages to a specific file convention like write_set: ["{target}NEWFILE_test.go"] with a placeholder the worker fills in. Needs design decision alongside enforcement work.

title: show command omits writing_style, personality, and skills fields
investigate Agent isolation:worktree behavior with ethos sub-agent types
description: During Phase 3.2 (ethos-07m.6, mission m-2026-04-08-001), the COO delegated bwk with Agent isolation:worktree and run_in_background:true. The agent's changes landed in main's working tree, not in .claude/worktrees/agent-XXXX/. git worktree list showed only main. Hypothesis (unverified): the worktree wrapper sets the agent's CWD but does not intercept absolute paths in tool calls. The COO's prompt used absolute paths throughout. Alternative: ethos sub-agent types may not honor isolation:worktree at all. This is a workflow primitive failure that compounded into COE-9 (concurrent file modification corrupted leader's reads) and is a latent risk for any future round where leader and worker share the same working tree. Run a controlled experiment: spawn a no-op ethos sub-agent with isolation:worktree, observe whether git worktree list shows the worktree, observe whether absolute-path Write calls land in main or the worktree. Document the finding as a memory entry and update the COE. Full COE entry at .tmp/coe/2026-04-08-phase-3.2-workflow-violations.md (COE-4).
close_reason: Investigation complete. DES-038 settled. isolation:worktree works as designed (scratch-and-merge, not same-branch isolation). Protocol: default OFF for single-worker feature delivery. See PR #188.

title: PreCompact hook uses invalid hookSpecificOutput schema — should use systemMessage
Generate .claude/agents/*.md from ethos identity data
description:
Generate .claude/agents/<handle>.md from ethos identity data, aligned with Claude Code's official sub-agent architecture. Full specification: ethos/docs/agent-identity-spec.tex (compiled PDF: agent-identity-spec.pdf).

## Summary

Three mechanisms deliver identity to sub-agents:
1. Agent definition file (static, generated by SessionStart) — frontmatter + personality body

Phase 1: baseline-ops skill (ethos-l9d)
Phase 2: SubagentStart extension fix (ethos-nri)
Phase 3: Agent definition generation (this bead)
Phase 4: Role-based hook generation
Phase 5: Rollout — replace hand-written agents across all repos

See agent-identity-spec.tex Sections 6-7 for full criteria.

title: refactor: hook standards compliance
Agent team teammates have no ethos session
description:
Agent team teammates have no ethos session.

PROVEN ROOT CAUSE: FindClaudePID() fails for teammates because the binary name is a version number, not 'claude'.

Evidence chain:
1. Hooks DO fire for teammates — quarry SessionStart, beads bd prime, and ethos SessionStart all fire. MCP servers and skills all load. (Tested with diagnostic teammates.)
2. The ethos hook receives a valid session_id in stdin payload: {"session_id":"92438591-...","hook_event_name":"SessionStart",...}
3. But FindClaudePID() in internal/process/tree.go calls isClaudeComm() which checks filepath.Base(comm) == "claude".
4. The lead's claude process was launched via symlink ~/.local/bin/claude → .local/share/claude/versions/2.1.86. ps -o comm shows 'claude'. isClaudeComm returns true. Session created.
5. The teammate's claude process was spawned directly by the lead's binary. ps -o comm shows '/Users/jfreeman/.local/share/claude/versions/2.1.86'. filepath.Base returns '2.1.86'. isClaudeComm('2.1.86') returns false.
6. FindClaudePID falls back to os.Getppid() — a transient PID that dies. Session PID file becomes orphaned. ethos session roster returns 'No active session.'

FIX: isClaudeComm (or the full path check in readProc) must also match version-named binaries under the claude versions directory. Biff solved the same problem in DES-011a (find_session_key in src/biff/session_key.py).

Specific code: internal/process/tree.go:64-70 — isClaudeComm needs to match paths containing '/claude/versions/' or version strings like '2.1.86' when the parent path is the claude versions directory.
close_reason: Fixed in PR #126. FindClaudePID now recognizes version-named Claude Code binaries on macOS and Linux.

title: release: treat HTTP 404 as success when deleting head branch after merge
description: When a repo has 'Automatically delete head branches' enabled, GitHub deletes the PR's head branch synchronously as part of the merge. The punt release merge helper then calls DELETE /repos/.../git/refs/heads/<branch> and gets HTTP 404, which it treats as an error and exits non-zero. Root cause: deletion is unconditional; the postcondition is 'branch does not exist' and 404 proves it. Fix: treat 404 as a success signal — change the success check from (200, 204) to (200, 204, 404). Observed during ethos v3.0.0 release: all 5 PRs merged successfully but punt exited code 1.

title: make check hook command — better default than tail -20 + blocking semantics
description:
Follow-up from ethos-9ai.2 round 1 review (mdm). The role-based hook injected in PR #TBD uses 'make check 2>&1 | tail -20' as the canonical post-write command. Two known weaknesses flagged for a future iteration:

1. tail -20 loses Go compile errors. Go compile errors fire first and short-circuit the rest of make check. A typical broken-build output is <20 lines and tail catches it fine, but a build that compiles and then fails 40 tests with -race -count=1 -v verbose output produces 500+ lines, and tail -20 shows the last test's trailing lines plus the make summary — not the first failing test. Better defaults: grep-filter actual error lines, tail -40, or head+tail split.

2. Exit code masked by pipe to tail. In POSIX sh the pipeline exit status is the last command's. tail -20 always exits 0 regardless of what make check did. The hook is advisory (visible output), not blocking — Claude Code cannot gate the next Write on a broken build. Either wrap in bash -o pipefail to make it blocking, OR soften docs/agent-identity-spec.tex §Role-Based Hooks 'behavioral enforcement' language to 'visible enforcement' to match reality.

Action: pick a default, update generate_agents.go and the canonical example in agent-identity-spec.tex §Role-Based Hooks + DOC-5 citation in sync. Also add one sentence to §Role-Based Hooks pinning shell to POSIX sh ('The command runs under /bin/sh; use POSIX-sh syntax only') so a future contributor does not add bashisms.

Source: mdm's round-1 review of commit 897cc87 on feat/role-based-hooks.

title: Integration design: ethos + quarry — agents with persistent memory
description:
Design and implement the integration between ethos and quarry. Dependency flows one way: quarry reads ethos, ethos never knows about quarry. This is the least proven integration — needs design exploration before implementation.

What it enables: agents have persistent memory across sessions. Past conversations, decisions, work artifacts, and reasoning are searchable and recallable as the agent's own experience. When Claude starts a new session, it can recall 'I worked on persona animation last week' from quarry.

Integration points:
- Quarry reads identity (handle) from ethos to scope memory to the agent
- Quarry stores collection name, search preferences, retention policy in ext/quarry on the identity
- Memory ingestion: session transcripts, bead history, commit messages, PR summaries attributed to the agent
- Memory retrieval: semantic search scoped to the agent's handle, surfaced at session start or on demand

Open questions:
- Per-agent collection vs. agent-scoped namespace within project collections?
- What gets ingested? Full session transcripts are large. Summaries? Key decisions? Bead close messages?
- How does memory survive across repos? Agent memory is personal, not project-scoped.
- Is quarry the right mechanism? It indexes files on disk today. Agent memory may need a different model (event stream vs. document corpus).
- How does the auto-memory system in Claude Code (.claude/projects/*/memory/) relate? Complement or replace?

Ext schema (ext/quarry):
- collection: quarry collection name for this agent's memory
- retention_days: how long to keep memory (0 = forever)
- auto_ingest: what to auto-capture (sessions, beads, commits, none)

Deliverable: design exploration doc, decision on mechanism, ext schema definition, implementation beads if quarry is confirmed as the right approach. No changes to ethos core.

title: mission: document escalate-with-empty-files-changed path in CLI help and delegation template
description:
Workers don't discover the escalate path for mid-round scope gaps. In the Phase 3 dogfood (PR #199, mission m-2026-04-09-006 → m-2026-04-09-007), bwk correctly stopped at a write_set boundary but had to report the blocker through the agent conversation channel because he didn't know that 'verdict: escalate' with 'files_changed: []' and the reason in open_questions was a valid shape. The result validator explicitly allows empty files_changed (internal/mission/result.go lines 161-164), but the CLI help shows only a verdict=pass example with a non-empty files list.

Scope:
1. Update 'ethos mission result --help' to include an escalate example with empty files_changed, populated open_questions, and prose explaining the blocker
2. Add a one-sentence note to 'ethos mission --help' pointing to the escalate protocol
3. Update the delegation template / contract.context convention so every new worker sees the escalate protocol in their contract before they hit a boundary

Out of scope: schema changes, new artifact types, new commands. The path already exists in the primitive; this is pure discoverability.

Sequencing: can ship any time after PR #199 lands. Low risk, self-contained, no dependencies.

title: resolve.ResolveAgent silently swallows LoadRepoConfig errors
description:
Discovered during ethos-9ai.6+9ai.7 round 2 C1 test (mdm's caller-propagation finding). resolve.ResolveAgent at internal/resolve/resolve.go:170-174 does:

  cfg, err := LoadRepoConfig(repoRoot)
  if err != nil {
    fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, "ethos: loading repo config: %v\n", err)
    return ""
  }

On a malformed .punt-labs/ethos.yaml (yaml parse error) or permission error, ResolveAgent logs the error to stderr and returns the empty string. HandleSessionStart then hits the agentPersona == "" early-return branch at session_start.go:110 and returns nil BEFORE GenerateAgentFiles is reached.

This means 9ai.6's config-load error propagation fix is invisible on the path most users will actually hit — if the user has a broken config, ResolveAgent swallows the error first. The only way to observe a malformed-config failure through HandleSessionStart is if some OTHER code path (team load, role load) happens to also fire and produce a different error.

9ai.6+9ai.7 round 2 uses the missing-team path instead of the malformed-yaml path in TestHandleSessionStart_GenerateAgentsErrorPropagates precisely because the malformed-yaml path is unreachable from HandleSessionStart.

Fix: propagate ResolveAgent errors to its callers instead of log-and-swallow. Two callers:
- HandleSessionStart at internal/hook/session_start.go:78 — should return the error up, same fail-closed semantics as the 9ai.6+9ai.7 round 2 C1 fix
- ethos resolve-agent CLI command (wherever it lives under cmd/ethos/) — should exit non-zero on config errors for diagnostic value

This closes the class 9ai.6 opened. Keep the shell wrapper's || true for fail-open on Claude Code session startup; the exit-code signal is for direct CLI invocation and unit tests.

Related: silent-failure hunter's L1 observation in the 9ai.6 round 1 review about the identity-load, role-load, and empty-tools/personality skip paths in the member loop. Those are a similar class (per-member silent skips) and could be rolled into the same bead if scope permits.

Source: bwk's round 2 report on commit 8cf476a, 'Torpedo alert' at the bottom (note: use direct language, not the torpedo word, in future).
close_reason: Shipped via PR punt-labs/ethos#194 (merged 2026-04-09, commit 12ad59e). resolve.ResolveAgent and resolve.ResolveTeam now propagate LoadRepoConfig errors via (string, error) signatures. Closes the silent-swallow class at the layer above 9ai.6 — a malformed .punt-labs/ethos.yaml is now visible end-to-end through ethos hook session-start (exit 1 with full 4-layer chain: 'ethos hook session-start: resolving agent: resolve agent: parsing repo config: yaml: ...'). This is the exact test case 9ai.6 round 2 had to substitute with missing-team because the path was unreachable. 3 bwk rounds + 1 local 4-reviewer cycle (djb iterate on doubled wrap, feature-dev approve with 2 Important, silent-failure hunter approve, mdm H1/M1 + follow-ups) + 1 Copilot PR cycle (3 stale-text findings from the round 1-to-2 rename, all fixed in round 3). Per-caller discipline: HandleSessionStart fail-closed, BuildTeamSection fail-open, runResolveAgent exit 1, CheckDefaultAgent diagnostic. Binary null-effect md5 hashes byte-identical across all rounds. Follow-ups: ethos-dkw (TestResolveAgentJSONFlag latent hard-exit under sandbox), ethos-f9n (BuildTeamSection silent-degradation visibility).

title: SessionStart hook error: installed plugin is v0.2.0, binary is v0.3.0
description: Root cause: punt release built and tagged v0.3.0 but did not update the marketplace. The installed plugin at ~/.claude/plugins/cache/punt-labs/ethos/0.2.0/ has old hooks without MCP auto-allow, dev mode detection, or new commands. The binary at ~/.local/bin/ethos is v0.3.0 with new session subcommands. Version mismatch causes session-start.sh to call session subcommands that exist in v0.3.0 binary but the hook itself is v0.2.0 code. Fix: update marketplace.json ref to v0.3.0 tag, then claude plugin update.
close_reason: Marketplace PR #79 merged, plugin now installs v0.3.0

title: Pipeline store — layered YAML store for pipeline declarations
description: internal/mission/pipeline.go + pipeline_test.go. Layered store for pipeline YAML files from ~/.punt-labs/ethos/pipelines/ and .punt-labs/ethos/pipelines/. Loads stage declarations, validates each stage's archetype exists in the archetype store. See docs/mission-pipelines.md for the full design.

title: TestResolveAgentJSONFlag latent hard-exit under test sandbox
description:
Follow-up from ethos-dc0 round 1 silent-failure hunter L1. Post-dc0, runResolveAgent calls os.Exit(1) on config errors. TestResolveAgentJSONFlag at cmd/ethos/main_test.go:95-105 runs rootCmd.Execute() for 'resolve-agent --json' and comments 'Execute may fail (no git repo) — that's fine'. Pre-dc0, runResolveAgent never exited on any error. Post-dc0, if the test environment walks up to a malformed config (unlikely today because the test cwd finds the real repo's valid .punt-labs/ethos.yaml), os.Exit(1) would terminate the test binary mid-run and mask every subsequent test result.

Not triggering today because:
- The test walks from cmd/ethos/ upward; FindRepoRoot lands on the real repo
- The real .punt-labs/ethos.yaml parses cleanly
- No CI sandbox, parallel run, or fuzz harness currently runs the test in a dir with a malformed config

Fix: either (a) refactor runResolveAgent to return an error and let main decide whether to Exit (cleaner signature), or (b) isolate the test environment with t.TempDir + fake config so it never touches the real repo's walk.

Source: silent-failure hunter on commit cd8ab4f.

Running 'ethos mission pipeline instantiate <name>' on a built-in fails validation because archetypes like implement/test/design require non-empty write_set. Update built-ins to declare write_set entries with {target} or similar convention so a user can run 'instantiate quick --var target=internal/foo/ ...' and get valid missions. Follow-up to ethos-ohx.

title: Design: mission archetypes as typed subtypes with authorization semantics
description: When beadle uses missions as the orchestrator control plane, the x bit (rwx permission model) maps to 'can this contact create this type of mission.' Archetypes become typed subtypes that constrain valid state: an email-triggered mission has different write_set rules, budget defaults, and authorization requirements than a code implementation mission. This is trust enforcement, not prompt guidance. Design the type field, archetype registry, per-archetype validation rules, and how archetypes interact with the beadle rwx model.

title: BuildTeamSection should surface ResolveTeam error into visible output
description:
Follow-up from ethos-dc0 round 1 mdm M2. BuildTeamSection in internal/hook/pre_compact.go is a sidecar called from HandlePreCompact. It's documented as fail-open: 'Returns empty string if teams is nil, no team is configured, or on any load error.' Post-dc0 it logs the ResolveTeam error to stderr and returns empty string.

The problem: pre-compact runs as a Claude Code hook, and its stderr goes to the Claude Code hook log — an operator has to know to look there. A broken .punt-labs/ethos.yaml silently drops the team context from post-compact output and the operator only notices when team section is missing, without knowing why.

Options:
- (a) Embed the error as a visible 'Team context: unavailable (broken config: ...)' section in the compaction context so the leader agent sees it
- (b) Add a CheckDefaultTeam doctor probe that mirrors CheckDefaultAgent so 'ethos doctor' catches the condition
- (c) Both

For dc0 this was accepted as out of scope because BuildTeamSection's documented contract is 'returns empty string on any load error' and changing it is a separate design decision.

Source: mdm round 1 review of commit cd8ab4f.

title: L4 behavioral test infrastructure
description: Fixture repo, test harness, and assertion framework for L4 agent behavioral tests. Foundation for all three layers: deterministic protocol checks, LLM-judged constraint compliance, adversarial scenarios.

title: Integration design: ethos + beadle — agents with email
description:
Design and implement the integration between ethos and beadle. Dependency flows one way: beadle reads ethos, ethos never knows about beadle.

What it enables: agents send and receive email as themselves. Claude Agento sends from claude@punt-labs.com with GPG signing. Each agent identity has its own email address, credentials, and contact permissions.

Integration points:
- Beadle reads identity (name, handle, email field) from ethos sidecar at known filesystem paths
- Beadle stores GPG key ID, IMAP config, trust preferences in ext/beadle on the identity
- Beadle resolves identity per-request — no cached state, always reads current ethos identity
- Multi-identity: different agents in the same session can send as different addresses

Ext schema (ext/beadle):
- gpg_key_id: GPG key fingerprint for signing
- imap_host, imap_port: mail server config (if not using Proton Bridge defaults)
- trust_default: default trust level for unknown senders

Existing state: beadle already has partial ethos integration (reads email field, uses ext/beadle for GPG). Needs audit for completeness — are all integration points using the sidecar contract correctly? Does multi-identity work end-to-end?

Deliverable: design doc, ext schema definition, audit of current integration, implementation beads for gaps. No changes to ethos core.

title: mission: pre-delegation lint (ethos mission lint <contract.yaml>)
description:
Catch write_set and success-criteria gaps at spec-write time rather than at worker-run time. Addresses the root cause of the Phase 3 dogfood escalation (PR #199) — the leader's initial write_set was missing an adjacent test file and README example lines, both of which could have been flagged by trivial heuristics.

Proposed checks (advisory, non-blocking):
- write_set contains a .go file but not the adjacent _test.go
- write_set contains production code but no CHANGELOG.md entry
- success_criteria mention README/docs but README.md is not in write_set
- write_set contains _test.go but no corresponding .go (inverted gap)
- inputs.files contains paths not in write_set (signal that leader may want to add them)
- evaluator identity has no role bound (pre-Phase-3.3 content hash failure path)

Command shape: ethos mission lint <contract.yaml> — parses, runs heuristics, prints warnings or 'no issues'. Does not require a store round-trip. Non-blocking (exit 0 even with warnings).

Defer until we have 2-3 more dogfood cycles of data about which heuristics are worth writing. Current evidence (one cycle) suggests adjacent test file and docs-in-success-criteria are the top two, but more cycles would refine the list.

title: Automatic mission traceability — auto-append summary JSONL on mission close
description: When ethos mission close runs, automatically append a summary line to <repo>/.ethos/missions.jsonl. One JSONL row per closed mission with id, timestamps, leader/worker/evaluator, ticket, write_set, success_criteria, rounds, verdict, files_changed. Append-only, git-tracked.

title: firstContentLine truncates multi-line sentences in persona opening
description: BuildPersonaBlock uses firstContentLine to extract the opening descriptor from personality markdown. When the first content line wraps across multiple lines (e.g., 'Principles from the Unix philosophy and\nMcIlroy'\''s work on software componentization.'), only the first line is extracted, producing a truncated sentence: 'Principles from the Unix philosophy and.' Fix: extract until the first sentence boundary (period followed by whitespace/newline/EOF) or until a blank line.

title: Symlink policy across mission loaders (defense in depth)
description: LoadEvents, LoadReflections, LoadResults, and Store.Load all follow symlinks via os.ReadFile without checking whether the target is inside missionsDir. An attacker with write access to ~/.punt-labs/ethos/missions/ can plant a symlink that redirects a reader at arbitrary host content. Mitigation: add an os.Lstat check before os.ReadFile that refuses symlinks whose resolved target is outside missionsDir. Apply to all four loaders in a single change. Deferred from Phase 3.7 round 2 (m-2026-04-08-006) per djb's recommendation — fixing one loader but not the others would create asymmetry worse than the current consistent weakness.

title: Phase 5: Ecosystem
description: Long-term investments: starter team templates, agent marketplace, cross-tool workflow orchestration. See docs/ETHOS-ROADMAP.md Phase 5.

title: Starter team template: sprint team (gstack-inspired workflow)
description:
Port gstack workflow discipline into ethos starter content + build repeatable Tier 2 ingestion process.

## Context

gstack is 23 Claude Code SKILL.md files (prompt-only, no code) by Garry Tan that simulate an engineering team. The port extracts the prompt quality into ethos structural layer: persistent identities, enforced roles, mission templates with write-set boundaries.

Tier 1 (batteries + create wizard) already ships — ethos seed + ethos create. Most CLAUDE.md files lack enough personal content for identity extraction. The work starts at Tier 2.

## Tiered ingestion model

### Tier 1 — Already shipped
ethos seed (6 roles, 10 talents, baseline-ops skill) + ethos create (4 prompts). 90% starter content, 10% user input. No parsing.

### Tier 2 — Specific importers (this bead)
Structured sources with enough content to extract real personas and constraints:
- gstack SKILL.md → 6 personas, 6 roles, safety constraints, mission archetypes (reference implementation)
- SoulSpec (SOUL.md, IDENTITY.md, STYLE.md) → identity + personality + writing style (future)
- CrewAI agent configs → roles, tools, delegation patterns (future)
- Subcommand: ethos import --from gstack|soulspec|crewai

### Tier 3 — LLM-driven (future bead)
Point ethos at any prompt files or workflow docs. LLM reads, identifies personas and constraints, proposes identities and roles for review. Skill: /ethos:import <path>.

## Deliverables

### Sprint team content (gstack reference implementation)

6 personalities, 6 writing styles, 6 roles with safety constraints, 1 team template (sprint.yaml), 4 mission archetype templates (design, implement, review, qa), sprint workflow doc.

Personas: product-thinker, architect, implementer, code-reviewer, qa-engineer, security-auditor.

### Repeatable process documentation
docs/ingestion.md — how to decompose any structured prompt collection into ethos identities, roles, and mission templates. The gstack port is the worked example.

### ethos import --from gstack
CLI subcommand that reads a gstack skills directory and generates ethos content files. Reference implementation for Tier 2 importers.

## Not in scope
- Tier 1 changes (already shipped)
- Tier 3 LLM-driven ingestion (separate bead)
- Browser automation (gstack /qa uses Playwright — ethos doesn't own browser tooling)
- Verbatim gstack prompt copying (extract the structure, write original ethos content)

## Acceptance criteria
- ethos seed deploys sprint team content (6 personas, 6 roles, 1 team, 4 templates)
- ethos import --from gstack reads SKILL.md files and generates ethos content
- docs/ingestion.md documents the repeatable extraction process
- docs/sprint-workflow.md explains the sprint sequence with mission examples
- make check passes

title: ethos create: LLM-assisted identity from project context (Tier 1 fine-tuning)
description:
Tier 1 ingestion enhancement. ethos create detects CLAUDE.md (and optionally .claude/agents/, agent memory) in the current repo and uses an LLM to fine-tune starter content defaults.

Not extraction — fine-tuning. The starter content (personalities, writing styles, talents) is the 90% base. The LLM reads project context and adjusts:

- Communication preferences (terse, formal, data-driven) → writing style refinement
- Coding conventions (type annotations, functional style) → talent/personality notes  
- Identity hints (Co-Authored-By, git config) → pre-fill name/email in wizard
- Agent definitions (.claude/agents/) → suggest role mappings

Flow: ethos create --from-project (or auto-detect CLAUDE.md during ethos create):
1. Read CLAUDE.md + .claude/agents/ + git config
2. LLM proposes fine-tuned identity: writing style adjustments, personality notes, talent suggestions
3. User confirms or adjusts each suggestion
4. Identity created with starter content base + project-specific refinements

Could also be a /ethos:create skill for richer LLM interaction.

Depends on: existing ethos create wizard, ethos seed starter content.

title: Design: SoulSpec as ingestion source, not interop format
description:
SoulSpec (SOUL.md, IDENTITY.md, STYLE.md, AGENTS.md) treated as an ingestion source — same pattern as CLAUDE.md (Tier 1) and gstack (Tier 2). ethos create detects SoulSpec files and proposes identity defaults. One direction: SoulSpec → ethos. No export, no format adoption, no bidirectional mapping.

Rationale: SoulSpec is markdown prose. Ethos's conviction is that prose alone doesn't enforce — typed contracts, schema validation, and hash-pinning are required for reliability. SoulSpec files are onboarding ramps, not interop obligations. Drop feat:soulspec-export from PR/FAQ Should Do.

Tier 2 importer: ethos import --from soulspec reads SOUL.md → personality, STYLE.md → writing_style, IDENTITY.md → name/handle, AGENTS.md → role tool hints. Produces ethos YAML identity with structural metadata SoulSpec can't express.

Resolves the SoulSpec integration strategy question raised during PR/FAQ v2.2 discussion.

title: Mission archetypes: named contract conventions (implement, design, review, investigate)
description: PR/FAQ feat:archetypes. Named shorthands that suggest contract conventions for each type of work. Not new schema — the archetype is expressed through contract specificity (tight vs broad write-set, specific vs open criteria). Deliverables: example contract templates per archetype, documentation explaining the spectrum, optional archetype field on mission contract (advisory, not enforced). See ethos-2br resolution: agency is contract specificity, not a mode switch.

title: Lossy export: ethos export --to soulspec|claude-md
description: PR/FAQ feat:lossy-export. Export ethos identities to less-structured formats for distribution and portability. ethos export --to soulspec produces SOUL.md, IDENTITY.md, STYLE.md, AGENTS.md. ethos export --to claude-md produces a CLAUDE.md personal section. Both exports are lossy by design: structural data (roles, teams, missions, safety constraints, frozen evaluator, audit trails) drops because the target format cannot represent it. The loss signals what ethos adds. Export is compilation from a richer system to a simpler one.

title: Integration guides for third-party tools
description: PR/FAQ feat:guides. Documentation and examples for third-party tools to read ethos identity and mission state via filesystem, CLI, or MCP. Three guides: filesystem sidecar (read YAML at known paths, zero dependency), CLI integration (shell commands from hooks/scripts), MCP integration (connect to ethos serve). Each guide includes a working example.

title: Git-tracked mission artifacts: contracts + results in repo
description:
Option B from the audit trail design discussion. Mission contracts and final results go into git (.ethos/missions/<id>.yaml, .ethos/missions/<id>.result.yaml). Detailed event logs and session audit stay local. The git history shows what was delegated and what was delivered. The local logs show how.

Intent capture: the contract carries success_criteria (what the leader wanted), the result carries verdict + evidence (what was delivered), the reflection carries reason (why the leader accepted or rejected). Together they document the full intent chain in version control.

Framed as a lightweight built-in alternative to Entire.io's commit-level governance checkpoints. Entire.io captures governance externally; ethos's mission lifecycle IS the governance, and the artifacts travel with the code.

Design questions to resolve:
- Path convention: .ethos/missions/ in repo root? Or a different location?
- Which artifacts: contract + result only? Or contract + result + reflection + ADR?
- Commit strategy: auto-commit artifacts with the code? Or separate housekeeping commits?
- Sensitive data: tool_input_preview stays local (session audit), not in git
- Migration: existing local-only missions get an export-to-repo command

title: ADRs as a typed ethos artifact
description:
Today ADRs are freeform markdown in DESIGN.md. They should be a typed ethos artifact with schema validation, linkable to missions and beads.

An ethos ADR would capture:
- Decision ID (auto-generated, like mission IDs)
- Status: proposed | settled | superseded
- Context: what prompted the decision (link to mission, bead, or PR)
- Decision: what was decided
- Alternatives rejected: what was considered and why not
- Author: ethos identity handle
- Date: RFC3339

Storage: .ethos/adrs/<id>.yaml in the repo (git-tracked). The ADR travels with the code it governs.

Connection to missions: a mission's reflection can reference an ADR ('this approach was chosen per ADR ethos-041'). An ADR can reference the mission where the decision was made ('decided during mission m-2026-04-10-003').

CLI: ethos adr create, ethos adr list, ethos adr show <id>
MCP: adr tool with create/list/show methods

This makes intent capture structural — not freeform markdown that may or may not exist, but a typed artifact that the mission lifecycle produces and the codebase carries.

title: Quarry integration: tag transcript chunks with active mission ID
description:
Quarry's PreCompact hook already captures conversation transcripts. When ethos has an active mission in the session, the captured chunks should be tagged with the mission ID so semantic search can find the reasoning behind specific missions.

Three-layer audit trail:
- Git (.ethos/): contracts, results, ADRs — structured, shared, permanent
- Local (~/.punt-labs/ethos/): event logs, session audit — operational, single machine
- Quarry (optional): full conversations tagged by mission — searchable by meaning

The integration is a tagging convention, not new code in either tool. When quarry captures a transcript, it reads the active mission ID from ethos (via session roster or MCP) and includes it as metadata on the ingested chunks. Search 'reasoning for mission m-2026-04-10-003' returns the conversation context.

Depends on: ethos-k28.7 (git-tracked artifacts) for the structured layer. Quarry adds the unstructured reasoning layer on top.

title: Create baseline-ops skill for sub-agent operational discipline
description:
Sub-agents that load via subagent_type get their agent definition as the ENTIRE system prompt, replacing Claude Code's default. They lose all generic operational guidance (tool usage, verification, output formatting).

The official mechanism to inject content at sub-agent startup is the skills frontmatter field. Create a baseline-ops skill that every generated agent preloads.

## Location

~/.claude/skills/baseline-ops/SKILL.md (user-level, available everywhere)

## Content (~50-80 lines)

1. Tool usage: Read over cat, Grep over grep, Glob over find
2. Verification: make check after every change, zero violations
3. No commits: return results to COO
4. Conventions: look at neighbors, follow existing patterns
5. Scope: no features beyond spec, no unnecessary comments
6. Security: no OWASP top 10 vulnerabilities, no logged secrets
7. Progress: use TodoWrite for multi-step work
8. Output: concise, no preamble

## Frontmatter

---
name: baseline-ops
description: Operational discipline for Punt Labs sub-agents
user-invocable: false
disable-model-invocation: true
---

## Verification

Spawn a sub-agent with skills: [baseline-ops] and ask how to read a file. It should say Read tool, not cat.

## Reference

See agent-identity-spec.tex Section 4.

title: SubagentStart hook does not inject extension session_context
description:
HandleSubagentStart (internal/hook/subagent_start.go) builds a persona block with personality, writing style, and talents but does NOT read extension session_context blocks from <handle>.ext/<tool>.yaml.

This means sub-agents do not receive:
- Quarry memory instructions (collection, search/remember commands, memory types)
- Vox voice configuration
- Beadle GPG key config
- Any future tool-scoped session context

The main session receives these via SessionStart (which calls BuildSessionContext including extensions). Sub-agents get only the persona block from SubagentStart.

## Fix

In HandleSubagentStart, after building the persona block:
1. Read all files in <handle>.ext/*.yaml
2. For each file containing a session_context key, append the value to additionalContext
3. If no extensions exist, skip silently

## Tests needed

- Identity with extensions → additionalContext includes session_context
- Identity without extensions → works as before
- Malformed extension file → skipped with warning, other extensions still processed

## Reference

See agent-identity-spec.tex Section 3.2.2 (Gap: Extension Session Context).

title: Align role output_format templates with strict mission.Result schema
description:
Follow-up from ethos-9ai.3 round 1 review (mdm HIGH-1). The sidecar role output_format templates (implementer, test-engineer, reviewer, architect, security-reviewer, researcher) describe a shape that matches the task-completion message convention used in Agent tool responses (informal 'RESULT: <task-id>' block format), not the strict YAML schema validated by mission.Result's DecodeResultStrict with KnownFields(true).

Four concrete drifts identified by mdm:
1. Top-level label 'RESULT: <task-id>' vs Result.Mission string (m-YYYY-MM-DD-NNN pattern)
2. Field 'worker:' vs struct field 'Author' (strict decode rejects unknown fields)
3. Missing 'round:' (required, 1-indexed, validated against mission.CurrentRound)
4. 'files_changed: - <path>: +N -M lines' vs FileChange struct {Path, Added: int, Removed: int}
5. 'evidence: - <check>: pass | fail' vs EvidenceCheck {Name, Status in {pass, fail, skip}}

Rejected in round 2 of ethos-9ai.3 as out of scope: the current templates intentionally match the task-completion convention (what workers emit in Agent tool responses, parsed by the leader by eye), not the mission result submit file format. The two are separate workflows. When mission result submit becomes the primary handoff path (currently Phase 3 plumbing exists but no live submit loop), re-align the templates to the strict YAML schema so a worker can copy the template body directly into a .results.yaml file and have it pass DecodeResultStrict.

Scope when scheduled:
- Update writer templates (implementer, test-engineer) to emit valid mission.Result YAML with mission/round/author/verdict/confidence/files_changed (structured)/evidence (structured)/open_questions
- Consider a similar strict YAML form for FINDINGS (no current consumer but establishes pattern)
- Add a round-trip test: parse each sidecar template's output_format body as YAML, decode as mission.Result, confirm Validate passes against a synthetic mission context

Source: mdm round 1 review of commit 109649c on feat/output-format.
close_reason: Shipped via PR #197 (merged 447b0d8). Writer role output_format templates (implementer, test-engineer) now emit valid mission.Result YAML that passes DecodeResultStrict + Validate. Round-trip tripwire test added in internal/seed/output_format_schema_test.go. Same-class drift sweep: ETHOS-ROADMAP.md §3.6 example, internal/role/store_test.go fixture, tightened negative-case assertion, open_questions instructional placeholder removed. Phase 2 strictly done after this.

title: write_set: reject BOM and zero-width Unicode in validateWriteSetEntry
description: djb's frozen-evaluator review of Phase 3.2 (ethos-07m.6) flagged a defense-in-depth gap: containsControlChar in internal/mission/validate.go only rejects C0 controls (<0x20) and DEL (0x7F), and strings.TrimSpace does not strip BOM (U+FEFF). A write_set entry like \ufeffinternal/foo would compare as distinct from internal/foo through the conflict check. Not exploitable on POSIX filesystems where these are genuinely distinct filenames, but a hardening item: extend validateWriteSetEntry to reject zero-width characters (BOM, ZWSP, ZWNJ, ZWJ) alongside control characters. Documented in DES-032 round 4 as a follow-up. djb verdict noted: 'a defense-in-depth hardening item, not a correctness bug in Phase 3.2's stated scope.'

title: Integration design: ethos + biff — agents with team messaging
description:
Design and implement the integration between ethos and biff. Dependency flows one way: biff reads ethos, ethos never knows about biff.

What it enables: agents appear in /who, send messages via /write, have real-time conversations via /talk, set plans via /plan, and are discoverable via /finger — all as their own identity.

Integration points:
- Biff reads identity (name, handle) from ethos sidecar for display name in /who and message attribution
- Biff stores preferred TTY, routing preferences, notification settings in ext/biff on the identity
- Session roster already bridges ethos and biff — biff reads participant list to know who is in the session
- /plan and /finger reflect the agent's current work and availability

Ext schema (ext/biff):
- tty: current TTY session name
- routing: message routing preferences (e.g., prefer /talk over /write)
- notifications: notification level (all, mentions, none)

Existing state: biff already reads the session roster and shows agents in /who. The ext/biff namespace exists. Needs audit: is /plan per-agent? Does /finger resolve identity correctly for agents? Does /talk work agent-to-agent?

Deliverable: design doc, ext schema definition, audit of current integration, implementation beads for gaps. No changes to ethos core.

mission: tool-level file allowlist enforcement for verifier isolation (SC6)
description: Phase 3.5 (ethos-07m.9) deliberately deferred success criterion 6 — mechanical file-allowlist enforcement — to a follow-up. The isolation block currently declares the allowlist as a prose directive. A misbehaving verifier subagent could still call Read, Grep, or Glob against a path outside the list. Phase 3.5's threat model was 'cooperating verifier, not adversarial'; prose is sufficient. For defense-in-depth against an adversarial verifier, add a PreToolUse hook handler that checks the tool call's target path against the allowlist derived from the mission's write_set + contract file. The allowlist needs to be communicated from SubagentStart (where the verifier spawn is detected) to PreToolUse (where the tool call happens), probably via an env var or a session-scoped scratch file. See DES-035 for the full rationale on why this was scope-deferred.

title: Pipeline CLI — ethos mission pipeline create/show/list
description: CLI surface for pipelines. ethos mission pipeline create <pipeline.yaml> creates missions for each stage, wiring outputs to inputs. ethos mission pipeline show <name> displays the pipeline. ethos mission pipeline list lists available pipelines. Supports --json. See docs/mission-pipelines.md.

title: Phase 4 status block drift in docs/agent-definitions.md and agent-identity-spec.tex
description:
Follow-up from ethos-b5g round 1 silent-failure hunter M2 and the same-class finding bwk flagged inline. Three stale 'Phase 4 not yet implemented' references remain across ethos docs even though ethos-9ai.2 shipped the role-based hook in Phase 2:

1. docs/agent-identity-spec.tex:698 — 'Status: Designed, not yet implemented' in the Phase 4 (Role-Based Hook Generation) section
2. docs/agent-identity-spec.tex:706-710 — 'role-based hooks (Phase 4) are not yet active, so generated agents rely on CLAUDE.md and agent definition bodies for operational guidance and behavioral enforcement'
3. docs/agent-definitions.md:87 — lists 'hooks' as 'Manual / future: Role-based'

All three are stale since 9ai.2 shipped. bwk left them out of ethos-b5g scope because the fix requires reconciling the Phase 4/Phase 5 status tracking story, which is broader than the hook-command-default bead.

These should be fixed as part of the Phase 2 documentation consolidation pass after the 4 Phase 2 follow-ups land (2z2 ✓, dc0 ✓, b5g in review, r6o next). Scope: update Phase 4 status from 'Designed, not yet implemented' to 'Shipped in ethos-9ai.2 (PR #190)'; rewrite the Phase 5 paragraph to reflect that hooks ARE now active; update the agent-definitions.md table entry from 'Manual / future: Role-based' to 'Generated from role.Tools'.

Source: silent-failure hunter round 1 on commit 33d4e83 on fix/hook-command-default, plus bwk's own inline torpedo flag in the same commit.
close_reason: Shipped via PR #196 (merged f8df17f). Phase 4 status block drift corrected in docs/agent-identity-spec.tex and docs/agent-definitions.md. Same-class fix: stale head -60 in CHANGELOG.md historical ethos-9ai.2 entry qualifier renamed to head -n 60.

title: Phase 1: Batteries Included — ship pre-built content
description: Ship content that makes ethos useful out of the box: baseline-ops skill, 6 starter roles, 10 starter talents, model field on Role. See docs/ETHOS-ROADMAP.md Phase 1.

title: Ship 6 starter roles (implementer, reviewer, researcher, architect, security-reviewer, test-engineer)
description: YAML files in sidecar/roles/, deployed by installer. Each role defines tools, responsibilities, and anti-responsibility patterns. See ETHOS-ROADMAP.md §1.2.

title: Ship 10 starter talents (code-review, testing, go, python, typescript, security, cli-design, api-design, documentation, devops)
description: Substantial markdown files (200-500 lines each) in sidecar/talents/, deployed by installer. Real domain expertise, not thin summaries. See ETHOS-ROADMAP.md §1.3.

title: Add model field to Role struct
description: Add Model string field to Role struct in internal/role/. GenerateAgentFiles includes model in frontmatter when non-empty. Default: inherit. See ETHOS-ROADMAP.md §1.4.

title: Installer deploys sidecar starter content to global ethos directories
description: install.sh seeds ~/.punt-labs/ethos/roles/ and ~/.punt-labs/ethos/talents/ with starter content from sidecar/. Also deploys baseline-ops skill to ~/.claude/skills/. Uses cp -n (no clobber) to preserve user customizations. Repo-local content in .punt-labs/ethos/ overrides global via layered store.
